nameMeanings.name: Verney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Varney
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from a French place called Vernay, probably chiefly Saint-Paul-de-Vernay (Calvados). The placename comes from a derivative of Gaulish verno- ‘alder’ + the locative suffix -acum.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Verney was born in 1188, in Bramshall, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1224, in his hometown, at the age of 36.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Verney est né en 1188, à Bramshall, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1224, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 36 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Verney wurde 1188 in Bramshall, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1224 im Alter von 36 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Verney è nato il nel 1188 nel Bramshall, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1224, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 36 anni.

ja.Bramshall, Staffordshire, England, Unite: イギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアブラムシャル
ja.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Verneyは1188年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアブラムシャルで生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1224年に故郷で36歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Verney(은)는 1188년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 브램샤에서 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1224년 그의 고향에서 36세의 나이로 사망했다.

mn.Bramshall, Staffordshire, England, Unite: Bramshall, Stæffordscīr, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Verney nasceu em 1188, em Bramshall, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1224, em sua cidade natal, com 36 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Verney родился в 1188 г. в местности брамшолл, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1224 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 36 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Verney nació en 1188, en Bramshall, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1224, en su pueblo, a la edad de 36 años.
